title: Deterministic stint-geometry windows
summary: F1 race strategy is organized into stints constrained by race distance and mandatory tyre-use rules.
rationale: F1 race strategy is organized into stints constrained by race distance and mandatory tyre-use rules. Current solutions estimate total laps and tyre finish margins, but they do not explicitly encode where the current stint lies relative to one-stop, two-stop, or three-stop race partition geometry.
implementation_hint: Create StintStartLap=LapNumber-TyreLife+1, StintProgressWithinRace, projected current-stint length if no stop, and distance-to-ideal stop laps for 1-stop, 2-stop, and 3-stop templates using EstimatedTotalLaps. Interact these distances with Stint, Compound, Is_WetWeather, and remaining mandatory stop/spec debt.
expected_effect: May capture planned strategy timing even before tyres look over-age, especially for early first stops and second-stop windows.
risk: Template assumptions can hurt unusual wet, safety-car, or testing rows; gate by wet/pre-season flags and let trees ignore weak templates.
